target.name: (-)-Myrioxazine A
target.motivation: Myrioneuron alkaloids are endowed with interesting biological activities, including antimalarial activity. The synthesis of (-)-Myrioxazine A demonstrates the utility of the developed methodology for accessing complex natural products.

success_criteria.free_text: Spectroscopic data and optical rotation matched those previously reported for the natural product.

key_disconnections.bond_description: C-N bond of the oxazinane ring
key_disconnections.reasoning: The oxazinane ring is formed in the final step from an 8-hydroxymethyl-DHQ precursor, which simplifies the synthesis by allowing the formation of the heterocyclic ring at a late stage.
Removal of the chiral auxiliary (2-phenylethanol moiety) is a common strategy to obtain the free amine, which can then be further functionalized or cyclized.

key_disconnections.bond_description: C-O bond of the oxazolidine ring
key_disconnections.reasoning: This disconnection allows for the stereoselective generation of the C8a stereocenter and removal of the chiral auxiliary.
Introduction of the hydroxymethyl group at C8 is achieved through a regioselective reaction with formaldehyde, which is a key step for accessing the Myrioneuron alkaloid scaffold.

retrosynthetic_strategy.rationale: The synthesis of (-)-Myrioxazine A leverages the versatility of perhydrooxazoloquinoline 2 as a common intermediate. The strategy involves regioselective introduction of a hydroxymethyl group at the C8 position, followed by stereoselective reductive cleavage of the oxazolidine ring and subsequent removal of the chiral auxiliary. The final step involves the formation of the oxazinane ring to yield the target natural product.

thought.background: Lactam 1 is a known starting material for DHQ alkaloids.
thought.gap: Need to convert lactam 1 into the reduced analogue 2, which is a latent form of an iminium salt and enamines, to enable the generation of quaternary stereocenters.
thought.decision: To obtain perhydrooxazoloquinoline 2, chose a two-step sequence: conversion of lactam 1 into the corresponding thiolactam followed by reduction with NaBH4, expecting an improved overall yield compared to previous work.

outcome.observation: Compound 2 was readily obtained in 84% overall yield from tricyclic lactam 1 by its conversion into the corresponding thiolactam followed by treatment with NaBH4.

evidence.text_span: Compound 2 was readily obtained in 84% overall yield10 from tricyclic lactam 1 by its conversion into the corresponding thiolactam followed by treatment with NaBH4 (Scheme 2).

thought.background: Perhydrooxazoloquinoline 2 can be considered a latent form of an iminium salt, allowing for nucleophilic additions.
thought.gap: Need to introduce a hydroxymethyl group at the C8 position of the DHQ nucleus, which is crucial for the synthesis of (-)-Myrioxazine A.
thought.decision: To introduce the hydroxymethyl group, chose formaldehyde as the electrophile, expecting regioselective addition.

outcome.observation: Refluxing a methanolic solution of perhydrooxazoloquinoline 2 with formalin for 24 h afforded a mixture of the expected C4a-substituted DHQ 12 along with its C8 regioisomer 13 in 51% yield (17:83 ratio) as the only stereoisomers detectable by spectroscopic methods.

evidence.text_span: Surprisingly, however, refluxing a methanolic solution of perhydrooxazoloquinoline 2 with formalin (CH2O 37% w/w in H2O solution) for 24 h afforded a mixture of the expected C4a-substituted DHQ 12 along with its C8 regioisomer 13 in 51% yield (17:83 ratio) as the only stereoisomers detectable by spectroscopic methods (Scheme 4).

thought.background: The reaction of 2 with formaldehyde at reflux yielded a mixture of regioisomers 12 and 13.
thought.gap: Need to improve the regioselectivity towards the C8 regioisomer 13, which is the precursor for (-)-Myrioxazine A.
thought.decision: To improve regioselectivity, chose to perform the reaction at room temperature, expecting kinetic control to favor one regioisomer.

outcome.observation: When the reaction was performed at room temperature, the angularly substituted compound 12 was regio- and stereoselectively isolated in 73% yield.

evidence.text_span: Interestingly, when the reaction was performed at room temperature, the angularly substituted compound 12 was regio- and stereoselectively isolated in 73% yield.

thought.background: Perhydrooxazoloquinoline 13 is an advanced precursor for (-)-Myrioxazine A.
thought.gap: Need to remove the 2-phenylethanol moiety (chiral inductor) and open the oxazolidine ring of 13 stereoselectively.
thought.decision: To achieve stereoselective reductive cleavage of the oxazolidine ring, chose DIBAL as the reducing agent, based on its known ability to coordinate with oxygen and deliver hydride from the same face.

outcome.observation: The reductive cleavage of the oxazolidine ring of 13 with DIBAL took place with excellent stereoselectivity and good yield to give cis-DHQ 27.

evidence.text_span: The reductive cleavage of the oxazolidine ring of 13 with DIBAL took place with excellent stereoselectivity and good yield to give cis-DHQ 27 (Scheme 7).

thought.background: Compound 27 contains the 2-phenylethanol moiety that needs to be removed.
thought.gap: Need to remove the benzylic substituent to obtain the free amine, 8-hydroxymethyl-DHQ 28.
thought.decision: To remove the benzylic substituent, chose catalytic hydrogenation conditions, which are standard for this transformation.

outcome.observation: The subsequent removal of the benzylic substituent under catalytic hydrogenation conditions afforded 8-hydroxymethyl-DHQ 28.

evidence.text_span: The subsequent removal of the benzylic substituent under catalytic hydrogenation conditions afforded 8-hydroxymethyl-DHQ 28.

thought.background: Alcohol 28 is a known synthetic precursor of several Myrioneuron-type alkaloids.
thought.gap: Need to cyclize 28 to form the oxazinane ring and complete the synthesis of (-)-Myrioxazine A.
thought.decision: To form the oxazinane ring, chose formalin in methanol, expecting a cyclization reaction.

outcome.observation: Treatment of 28 with formalin in methanol provided (-)-myrioxazine A. The spectroscopic data of our synthetic (-)-myrioxazine A matched those previously reported for the natural product, and its optical rotation was [α]D20 = -24.4 (c 1.2, MeOH).

evidence.text_span: In our hands, treatment of 28 with formalin in methanol provided (−)-myrioxazine A. The spectroscopic data of our synthetic (−)-myrioxazine A matched those previously reported for the natural product,18 and its optical rotation was [α]D20 = −24.4 (c 1.2, MeOH), [(+)-myrioxazine A18 [α]D20 = +21 (c 1.0, MeOH)].

Overall Yield.interpretation: Calculated from the yields of steps 1, 4, 5, and 6 (0.84 * 0.80 * 0.51 * 0.92 = 0.316)

Longest Linear Steps.interpretation: Steps 1, 4, 5, 6 form the longest linear sequence to the target.

Total Steps.interpretation: Total number of distinct experimental steps described.

validation.comparison_to_prior: The spectroscopic data and optical rotation of the synthetic (-)-myrioxazine A matched those previously reported for the natural product.
validation.significance: The synthesis provides an expeditious route to the Myrioneuron-type alkaloid (-)-myrioxazine A, demonstrating the utility of the perhydrooxazoloquinoline 2 intermediate for accessing DHQs with C8a aza-quaternary or C4a all-carbon quaternary stereocenters. The work also clarifies mechanistic intricacies of Michael additions to these systems and highlights the stereoselective control achieved with DIBAL reductions.

source.title: Enantioselective Access to Decahydroquinolines Bearing a C<sub>4a</sub> or C<sub>8a</sub> Quaternary Stereocenter from a Common Intermediate Total Synthesis of (-)-Myrioxazine A.
source.authors: Calbó A, Piccichè M, Rubert L, Comas-Iwasita E, Griera R, Estarellas C, Luque FJ, Bosch J, Amat M.
